items.title: Coty exits Gucci license early
items.summary: Manila Times (via GlobeNewswire) reports Coty will return the Gucci Beauty license to Kering roughly a year early for about $400 million, with Coty continuing to operate the business through June 30, 2027 while L'Oreal prepares to take over. An insider quoted says Kering is essentially paying Coty to keep L'Oreal happy.

items.title: L'Oreal enters 50-year exclusive worldwide beauty licence with Kering for Gucci
items.summary: Wire story picked up by Manila Times confirms L'Oreal's 50-year global license for Gucci fragrance and beauty, effective July 2027, moved forward one year from the original Coty contract expiry, as part of the Kering-L'Oreal beauty and wellness alliance announced in 2025.

items.title: Gucci and L'Oreal reach 50-year licensing accord
items.summary: Philippine business desk coverage of the same Gucci-L'Oreal beauty licensing deal, framed as international business news with no distinct local market angle added.

items.title: Inside Gucci's Generation Gucci Event in Manila
items.summary: L'Officiel Philippines covers a Manila event where local celebrities including Pia Wurtzbach-Jauncey, Kylie Verzosa and Michelle Dee wore looks from Demna's debut Pre-Fall collection for Gucci, part of the brand's regional activation push.

items.title: Coty to Get $400 Million for Early Exit From Gucci Beauty Deal
items.summary: Bloomberg reports market reaction to the Gucci beauty license transition: Kering shares fell about 3% and L'Oreal about 1.5% on the news, while analysts said the deal removes timing uncertainty but does not solve Coty's EBITDA gap once L'Oreal takes over.

items.title: Travel influencer's parents' price-shock reaction inside a Gucci store in Thailand goes viral
items.summary: A travel-creator reel showing an Indian family's astonished reaction to Gucci handbag prices (about 72,000 baht) inside a Thailand store passed 1.2 million views within two days; comment section was largely amused/affectionate rather than brand-critical, illustrating price-shock as a recurring SNS content format around Gucci in SEA tourist retail.

items.title: "Gucci = Bad Quality" discussion thread continues on PurseForum
items.summary: Long-running global PurseForum thread with continued recent activity airing quality complaints about Gucci leather goods -- cracking leather after warranty expiry, chipped hardware within months -- used here as an ongoing critical-angle community barometer since region-specific SEA forums yielded limited fresh Gucci quality discussion this cycle.

items.title: New energy at the Gucci Ho Chi Minh City store
items.summary: Vietnamese luxury lifestyle outlet luxuo.vn covers the renovated Gucci flagship on Dong Khoi in Ho Chi Minh City, a 700+ sqm space redesigned around Gucci's contemporary Italian identity with LEED-aligned sustainability features, positioned as an investment signal in the Vietnam market.
